title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our authentic Indian recipe. Creamy, rich, and bursting with flavor. Perfect for a delicious meal!
longTailKeywords: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani recipe
lowCompetitionKeywords: restaurant style Butter Chicken at home
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with cilantro and served with naan.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken, an authentic Murgh Makhani experience.

description: Savor the rich and creamy delight of classic Butter Chicken, known as Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian dish features succulent chicken pieces simmered in a luscious tomato-based gravy, enriched with butter, cream, and aromatic spices. It is a comforting and flavorful curry that promises an unforgettable culinary experience, perfect for any occasion.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: or Kashmiri chili for vibrant color
soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with plain yogurt, 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat all the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours for best flavor. (This is part of prep time).

instructions.title: Sear the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp oil in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Sear until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Remove the cooked chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add 1 tbsp more butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and saute until translucent and lightly golden, about 5 minutes. Add the remaining 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, and salt to taste. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 4-5 minutes until the tomatoes have softened and the mixture thickens slightly.

instructions.title: Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: Remove the pan from heat and let the gravy mixture cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender. Add 1/2 cup of water and blend until completely smooth and creamy. If you prefer an extra silky texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.

instructions.title: Simmer the Butter Chicken
instructions.text: Return the pureed gravy to the pan and place it over medium-low heat. Add the seared chicken pieces to the gravy. Stir in the remaining 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp sugar (if using), and bring to a gentle simmer. Cook for 10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors of the gravy and ensuring it is fully cooked through.

instructions.title: Finish and Garnish
instructions.text: Stir in the heavy cream and the remaining 2 tbsp of butter until melted and well combined. Gently crush the Kasuri Methi between your palms and add it to the curry. Simmer for another 2-3 minutes, just until heated through, but do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro before serving.

tips: For an authentic restaurant-style silky gravy, ensure you blend the tomato-cashew base until absolutely smooth. Passing it through a fine-mesh sieve after blending can remove any remaining bits.
Do not boil the curry after adding the heavy cream, as this can cause the cream to curdle. Always simmer gently on low heat until warmed through.
Kashmiri red chili powder provides a beautiful deep red color without excessive heat. If unavailable, use regular red chili powder sparingly based on your spice preference.
article:
Butter Chicken, known in its authentic form as Murgh Makhani, is more than just a dish; it's a global culinary icon. Originating from the bustling streets of Delhi, this beloved Indian chicken recipe has captivated palates worldwide with its rich, creamy texture, subtly spiced tomato gravy, and succulent pieces of marinated chicken. It represents the pinnacle of comfort food, a harmonious blend of sweet, tangy, and savory notes that dance on the tongue.

While many might consider it a dish best enjoyed in restaurants, the truth is that a truly exceptional Butter Chicken can be made right in your own kitchen. Imagine the aroma filling your home, promising a restaurant-quality meal without leaving your dining room. This article will guide you through crafting the classic Murgh Makhani, ensuring you achieve that perfect balance of flavors and tenderness that defines this extraordinary dish. Get ready to embark on a culinary journey that will elevate your home cooking and impress anyone lucky enough to share a meal with you.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, literally translates to "chicken with butter," and this name perfectly encapsulates its essence. Its origins trace back to the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi in the 1950s, where leftover tandoori chicken was combined with a rich, tomato-based gravy made with butter and cream to prevent it from drying out. The result was an instant sensation, creating a dish that quickly became a staple of Indian cuisine.

At its heart, Butter Chicken features tender pieces of chicken (often thigh meat for maximum juiciness) marinated in yogurt and spices, then either grilled, pan-fried, or baked until lightly charred. This cooked chicken is then simmered in a velvety smooth gravy crafted from puréed tomatoes, onions, ginger, garlic, and a delicate blend of aromatic spices like garam masala, fenugreek (kasuri methi), and a touch of chili. The generous addition of butter and fresh cream at the end gives it its characteristic richness and glossy finish, making it irresistibly luscious. It’s a dish renowned for its mild heat, allowing the complex flavors of the spices and the sweetness of the tomatoes to shine through, creating a truly unforgettable experience.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Murgh Makhani requires a selection of fresh, quality ingredients. Don't skimp here, as each component plays a vital role in building the layered flavors of this iconic dish.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, less heat)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   Salt to taste

For the Gravy:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 can (400g) crushed tomatoes or 3-4 ripe fresh tomatoes, puréed
*   1/2 cup cashews, soaked in hot water for 15 minutes (for creaminess)
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1 teaspoon cumin powder
*   1 teaspoon coriander powder
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon sugar (balances the acidity of tomatoes)
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   1/2 cup heavy cream
*   1 tablespoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi), crushed
*   Salt to taste

For Garnish:
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   A swirl of heavy cream

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps carefully to create an authentic and delicious Butter Chicken.

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring the chicken is evenly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, to allow the flavors to penetrate.

2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat a tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter and 1 tablespoon of oil over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and softened, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

4.  **Add Tomatoes and Spices:** Pour in the crushed tomatoes (or fresh tomato purée). Add red chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, and garam masala. Cook the mixture for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes break down and the oil starts to separate from the edges.

5.  **Blend the Gravy:** Drain the soaked cashews and add them to the tomato mixture. Stir well. Carefully transfer the entire gravy mixture to a blender. Blend until absolutely smooth, adding a little water or broth if needed to help it along. This step is crucial for the silky texture of the Butter Chicken gravy.

6.  **Assemble and Simmer:** Return the smooth puréed gravy to the pan. Add 1/2 cup of water or chicken broth and the sugar. Bring to a gentle simmer. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces.

7.  **Finish with Cream and Kasuri Methi:** Reduce the heat to low. Stir in the heavy cream and the crushed kasuri methi. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the gravy to thicken slightly. Taste and adjust salt if necessary.

8.  **Garnish and Serve:** Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and a final swirl of heavy cream.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality Butter Chicken often comes down to a few key techniques and considerations:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't rush the marination process. Allowing the chicken to soak in the yogurt and spices for at least 2 hours, or even overnight, will result in incredibly tender and flavorful chicken that truly stands out in the Murgh Makhani.
*   **Smooth Gravy is Essential:** The hallmark of a great Butter Chicken is its silky, smooth gravy. After cooking the tomato-onion base, blend it thoroughly until there are no discernible lumps. A high-speed blender works best, and you can even pass it through a fine-mesh sieve for ultimate smoothness if desired.
*   **Don't Overcook the Chicken:** The chicken is cooked twice – once before adding to the gravy and again during the final simmer. Be careful not to overcook it in the initial step, as it will continue to cook in the gravy. Overcooked chicken can become dry and tough.
*   **Use Quality Tomatoes:** The flavor of your tomatoes significantly impacts the gravy. If using canned, opt for good quality crushed or puréed tomatoes. If using fresh, ensure they are ripe and flavorful.
*   **Balance Flavors:** The addition of sugar helps balance the acidity of the tomatoes. Taste the gravy before adding the cream and adjust spices or sweetness as needed. The kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves) adds an authentic, slightly bitter, and aromatic depth that is characteristic of Butter Chicken, so don't skip it!

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments. For the ultimate Indian dining experience, serve your homemade Murgh Makhani hot with:

*   **Naan Bread:** Warm, fluffy naan is perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich gravy. Garlic naan or butter naan are particularly delightful choices.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice provides a light, aromatic counterpoint to the creamy curry, soaking up the flavors beautifully.
*   **Roti or Chapati:** For a healthier option, whole wheat roti or chapati also work wonderfully.
*   **Side Salad:** A simple green salad with a light vinaigrette can provide a refreshing contrast.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based dip, such as cucumber raita, can help balance the richness of the curry.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is perfect as is, you can certainly adapt it to suit different dietary needs or preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** Replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or even chickpeas for a delicious vegetarian version. Simply pan-fry your chosen substitute before adding it to the gravy.
*   **Dairy-Free Option:** For a dairy-free alternative, use coconut cream or full-fat coconut milk instead of heavy cream and a plant-based butter substitute. The flavor profile will shift slightly, but it will still be incredibly rich and satisfying.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** If you have a charcoal grill, cooking the marinated chicken over charcoal before adding it to the gravy will impart an authentic smoky flavor, mimicking traditional tandoor-cooked chicken.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to your preference. For a milder dish, use Kashmiri chili powder, which offers vibrant color with less heat. For more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper.

Making classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) at home is a rewarding experience that connects you with the heart of Indian cuisine. With its tender chicken enveloped in a luxurious, creamy tomato gravy, this dish is a testament to the magic of simple ingredients transformed into something extraordinary. Follow this guide, take your time with each step, and you'll soon be savoring a homemade Butter Chicken that rivals any restaurant creation. Enjoy the process, and most importantly, enjoy the delicious results!
